item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yikes

item.title: Re: Reworking
item.content:encoded:
I'm actually considering having a tale as a supplement detailing what happened to Miss Wood as she was changed, perhaps a diary they found in her possessions after her disappearance that should ramp up the horror a bit.
As for the horror, I guess I feel it's scarier for having based it off my old, abusive workplace haha.

item.title: Re: Reworking
item.content:encoded:
You've fixed most of the issues I had with this the first time around, I feel.

While its still not exactly a horror article for me as a reader, its at least now much more interesting, with some better imagery and a little more mystery.

The note at the end is kind of cheesy, but hey, not a deal breaker.

item.title: Reworking
item.content:encoded: Announcing that I have tweaked the article, expanding on several elements and taking into consideration the feedback I've gotten from you all. Better late than never right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are soylent people made of green?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A generic monster with a bare-bones backstory. Admittedly, it doesn't need some grand story or character arc, a generic monster could work even if played very straight, but the whole article feels underdone.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…
Is soylent green people?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I agree with you with the benefit of hindsight. If you have any advice I'd love to hear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As an old-school orginal creepypastoid monster this was a surprisingly simple entry (much like "Rachel Parks") but I think it doesn't quite work as is. I didn't really feel especially creeped out reading this and the reveal is too pat. This has potential though, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Having seen all this feedback, that's pretty much the plan, yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't find this scary at all, but I really like the imagery (and it does have a slight creep factor). However, I can't upvote because you didn't really do much with the concept. The addendum revealing that the entity is some random groundskeeper guy left me feeling especially cold, and almost made me downvote — it's a completely uninteresting reveal.

(EDIT: Specifically, the problem is that it's treated as the climax of the SCP. If it were stated matter-of-factly early on, that would be fine. Though it also currently doesn't seem like a relevant detail and might be less interesting than what we might otherwise imagine.)

I'd keep the concept and imagery and rework this later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take this as an IOU for one vote after the contest period ends. Did this scare me? Not really. But I did find it incredibly charming. As other commenters have said, I don't feel like this has the "punch" to be an X000, but I still think there's enough here to make it a good addition to the main list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is good as a regular SCP, but i just didn't feel the horror, i don't think this is worthy of the SCP 3000, but this is still is a good SCP, soo….

No Vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't mind the Scip. The event, and routine/explanation is good.

But I don't see a hook worthy of x000, particularly 3000(Horror).

Maybe if the story of the tattooed groundskeeper was somehow developed, there might be a link. No vote right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unfortunately I'm not really feeling this. The the plant monster is not particularly interesting in my opinion, and I feel the role of supernatural gardener has been done before, and is not particularly interesting here.

On top of that, the part that I felt served as the most promising source of horror (the monster turning people into fertilizer) was glanced over and thus the article just doesn't come across as that scary, or even that creepy for me as a reader.

There is certainly fertile ground (pun intended) for this to be turned into a proper horror article, but as is, this just falls flat.

-1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not quite a horror skip, and as a regular one, I don't know. I don't think I'm getting enough story here, though one is certainly suggested. It comes off as "creepy place with a thing that kills you if you bother it".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks for the suggestions and grammar fixes, they've been very helpful. I'm taking the time between now and the end of the contest where skips are put on the main list to come up with the best title I can

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks man, I just took it on my phone so I didn't think I'd need to resize. I'll get around to sorting that out when I get home

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed a few missing capital letters.

I like this, but it doesn't really feel big enough to be an X000 to me. I'll gladly upvote it after the contest, though.

+ Nitpicky stuff
- hide

"Upon manifesting, SCP-3000-A will run through a routine composed of watering the plants, meticulously searching for any sign of pests such as caterpillars or mold, pest extermination, temperature regulation, mulching collected cadavers into fertilizer, application of fertilizer, followed by watering once more. It has been seen emitting a potent hereby unknown pesticide in the form of a gas in aid of the latter task."

I think that list is too long to just refer to one of the items as "the latter task," especially since pest extermination (which I assume is what it's using the pesticide for) is actually closer to the beginning of the list. I would either make that the last item in the list and replace "latter" with "last," or just say "in aid of pest control."

"SCP-3000-A has proven capable of lifting weights in excess of 600 kilograms through use of muscles reinforced with plant cells more resistant to tearing"

How do we know this? Either there should be something about how we managed to get a tissue sample from this thing, or anything that we can't tell from observing it at a distance should be unknown.

Also, um, are you gonna give it a title?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That picture is incredibly big for something that's shrunk down to 300px, and it's also 4MB so that sucks for people with limited data. You might want to replace it with this, or make a smaller version yourself.

I'll read the SCP later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an idea I had the other morning heading into work at about 5:30AM where I took the photo above to show my freiends how inviting it was, and then throughout the day the idea slowly formed.
I'd like to thank Roget for his extensive work in helping me refine the idea, as well as Ihpkmn and Soulless for proofreading and helping the words flow smoother.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It's got nice imagery, you feel sad for this creature twisted to a horrible form and driven to uncontrollable rages who just wants to take care of their plants in peace. Eerie and melancholy. I like it +1
